method: within-word uniform-permutation null (E024 reused) + adversarial leave-top-k-word-type-out; LB positive control with productive-vs-few-type separation; comparator type-diversity sweep

positive_control.selection_rule: PRODUCTIVE = globally-sig sign with most distinct initial types; FEW-TYPE = globally-sig sign with highest top-type-share

key_findings: A-initial preference is HIGHLY type-diverse: 115 distinct word-types across 155 A-initial words (top type = 11/155 = 7.1% of A-initial words).
LEAVE-TOP-TYPE-OUT: removing the single most-frequent A-initial word-type (11 occ) leaves A-initial preference highly significant (p=0.0010, 144/1358 remaining).
Leave-top-2 (p=0.0010) and leave-top-3 (p=0.0010) also remain significant.
A-'s type-diversity (115 distinct initial types) far exceeds the comparator median (36.0); comparators: {'A': 115, 'KU': 33, 'I': 54, 'JA': 51, 'DA': 36, 'SA': 26}.
LB positive control PASSED: productive sign 'A' survives leave-top-type-out (p=0.001) while few-type sign '*63' collapses (p=0.001 -> 0.1039, top-type share 84%).
A- spans 40 distinct second-signs, indicating it attaches to many distinct continuations (not one stem).
successor_hypotheses: E026: Test whether A- attaches productively to specific second-sign CLASSES (e.g. does the second-sign distribution differ from the global word-internal distribution?) — a positional-slot-with-substructure test.
E027: Cross-script comparison — does Linear B show an analogous high-type-diversity word-initial slot, and does it survive leave-top-type-out? (productivity as a cross-script structural universal).
E028: Test A- productivity by site/support sub-corpus: is the high type-diversity stable across Haghia Triada vs other sites, or concentrated in one archive?

E029: Bigram-level analysis — is the A- + second-sign transition distribution uniform or structured (are there preferred A-X continuations)? This distinguishes 'free prefix' from 'constrained combinatorics'.
E030: Adversarial test on the SECOND-sign position — is there a productive second-slot, or is positional structure confined to position 1?
E031: Frequency-spectrum test — does the A-initial type-count distribution follow a power law (productive morphology) vs a few-type spike (lexicalized)?
non_circular: Sign names are ANONYMOUS; no phonetic value/sound/meaning/language/reading assigned to ANY sign. Positional/structural statistics ONLY (L2/L3). LB used as positive-control benchmark ONLY. Metric = count of >=2-sign words whose first sign is A under within-word uniform-permutation null.
deviations: machinery.py lb_positive_control selection logic was corrected after initial self-check: the first version selected the few-type candidate by top-share without requiring global significance, picking a non-significant sign (PTE) that could not demonstrably collapse. Corrected version selects FEW-TYPE from globally-significant signs by highest top-type-share (picked *63, which collapses p=0.001->0.104). prereg.md and plan_hash were frozen BEFORE this correction and were NOT changed; only machinery.py (an implementation detail) was refined. The frozen verdict rule in prereg step 6 was applied exactly as written.
